@@ -244,2 +244,20 @@
   - Held capacity at tier 5; current usage is well below the 25M/day limit.
 - Watch next week: whether A $99 now holds conversion with higher quality, cash after tier 4 R&D, issue age under heavier volume, compute, and tier 5 headroom.
+
+## Week 16 / Day 105
+
+- Cash: $2,142,254. Subscribers: 73,386. Week 15 added 22,398 subscribers from 39,488 leads; cash increased by $613,333 after the prior tier 4 R&D spend.
+- A $99 worked with higher quality. S2 content produced 34,991 A leads and 19,977 A subscribers, about 57.1% conversion.
+- Week 15 cash flow: subscription payments $2,484,057; compute -$425,342; ops -$388,500; ads -$280,000; capacity -$70,000; dev -$20,300; lead acquisition -$19,915.
+- S2 market estimate at info level 1: WTP ~$178.59, market cap ~167,374, current subs already 73k, so saturation will matter soon. Macro is contracting.
+- Issues: S2 has 6,526 open issues, avg age 0.32 days, max 48. Average handling remains fresh, but raw volume is high.
+- Changes:
+  - Started R&D tier 5 for $833,333. Sampled expected completion day 135, expected boost +0.0840.
+  - Ran 4 market discovery attempts; discovered D_E09 Real Estate Groups, an enterprise segment with estimated WTP ~$106 and quality floor ~0.319 but small cap ~54 firms. No enterprise ads yet.
+  - Raised prices to A $109, B $269, C $499, testing whether S2 quality now supports a higher A price.
+  - Raised quotas to A 30000, B 120000, C 300000.
+  - Scaled S2 content ads to $60,000/day.
+  - Increased ops to $76,500/day total: global $15,000, S1 $1,000, S2 $60,000, S3 $500.
+  - Increased S2 dev slightly; total dev is $3,400/day.
+  - Held capacity at tier 5; current average usage is ~10.1M/day vs 25M/day capacity.
+- Watch next week: A $109 conversion, S2 market saturation, tier 5 headroom after ad scale, issue age under higher load, and cash rebuild after tier 5 R&D.
